ORCHID PHARMA: SIGNS RUSSIA LICENSING & SUPPLY DEAL FOR EXBLIFEP® 🇷🇺💊

• 🤝 Signed an exclusive licensing & supply agreement with Pharmasyntez JSC to commercialize Exblifep® (Cefepime/Enmetazobactam) in Russia, subject to regulatory approval

• 🦠 Exblifep® is a novel antibiotic for complicated urinary tract infections (cUTI) and hospital-/ventilator-associated bacterial pneumonia (HAP/VAP) caused by Gram-negative pathogens

• 🌍 Pharmasyntez will hold exclusive rights to register and commercialize the drug in Russia, while Orchid will supply the finished dosage form

• 💰 Partnership represents a potential opportunity of ~US$178 million over the first 10 years

• ✅ Exblifep® is approved by the USFDA and EMA, and is the first new chemical entity (NCE) from an Indian pharma company to receive approvals from both regulators

🟢 Impact: Positive (Expands Orchid Pharma's global commercialization footprint and opens a significant long-term revenue opportunity.)

DILIP BUILDCON: ISSUES STATEMENT ON WAYANAD TUNNEL LANDSLIDE 🚧🌧️

• 🚨 Dilip Buildcon said its immediate priority is supporting rescue and relief operations following the landslide at the Anakkampoyil–Kalladi–Meppadi tunnel project site in Wayanad, while cooperating with district authorities and emergency agencies. 

• 🌧️ The company attributed the incident to an active monsoon spell with exceptionally heavy rainfall, noting that Wayanad received around 265 mm of rain in the preceding 24 hours, as per preliminary data from the Kerala State Disaster Management Authority.

• 🤝 The company said it will fully cooperate with the investigation and continue to provide verified updates as information becomes available. 

🟡 Impact: Neutral (The disclosure is an update on the incident and rescue efforts. The financial or operational impact cannot be assessed at this stage.)

Tam for EMS industry 

India is emerging as a key global electronics manufacturing hub, with the EMS industry expected to grow at 25-30% CAGR over FY24-29E. 

Growth is driven by the China+1 shift, electronics exports reaching ~USD48b in FY26 (+25% YoY), and policy support through PLI, INR400b ECMS and semiconductor initiatives. 

With India accounting for only 4-5% of the global EMS market, increasing component localization and export-led manufacturing provide a long runway for growth.

EMS companies which will go backwards into component manufacturing are the ones where complete integrated business models will be created

Business Updates

LME Approval
- Kutch Copper Ltd. (KCL), a wholly owned subsidiary of Adani Enterprises, has received London Metal Exchange (LME) brand registration.
- The approval covers 'Adani Copper' Grade-A copper cathodes.
- LME warrants will be available from July 10, 2026.
- The registration allows delivery against LME Copper futures contracts.

Strategic Significance
- KCL operates a 0.5 MTPA copper smelter at Mundra.
- It is among the world's largest single-location custom copper smelters.
- LME registration enhances global market access and international credibility.
- It also improves financing flexibility and strengthens India's position in the global copper supply chain.

Key Takeaway
- LME registration is a significant milestone that enhances Kutch Copper's global acceptance and supports Adani's long-term growth in the copper business.

reports.text:
EMKAY ON TYRE STOCKS 

We upgrade Apollo/CEAT to BUY from Add/Reduce and reiterate BUY on JK Tyre, owing to increased confidence in recovery of industry earnings amid a stronger demand/pricing environment.

Strong demand / Pricing enviornment to drive H2 recovery*

Over the last few months, tyre stocks have corrected sharply (~12-32%) from recent peaks on concerns around demand slowdown and sharp margin pressure from elevated crude and rubber prices. 

While near-term margins (H1 subdued, with Q1, the bottom quarter) will remain under stress, we believe the market is undermining the sharp recovery in the industry's earnings cycle. 

We remain constructive on the tyres space, supported by a confluence of factors: sustained strong replacement demand triggered by GST cuts; strong recovery in OEM demand; pricing discipline with ~8–9% cumulative price hikes taken by the industry over the past 3–4M aided by tight demand-supply dynamics; peaking of the RM basket with crude reverting to earlier levels; and sharp valuation correction. 

We raise FY28E EPS by ~17-28% and lift our TPs to Rs550 (from Rs425) for *Apollo Tyres* (16x FY28E PER), Rs4,500 (vs Rs3,600) for *CEAT* (18x FY28E PER vs 20x earlier), and Rs650 (from Rs550) for *JK Tyre* (14x FY28E PER), driven by strong demand and improved pricing outlook.

**MANKIND PHARMA | InCred View**

**Key Highlights**
- ADD rating initiated
- Target Price at ₹2,812
- Strategic business transformation
- Focus on R&D and niche therapies
- BSV acquisition strengthens portfolio
- Long-term growth opportunity

**Brokerage Updates**
- **Rating:** **InCred** initiated coverage with an **ADD** rating and a **Target Price of ₹2,812**.
- **Business Transformation:** The brokerage believes Mankind Pharma is **transitioning from a volume- and distribution-led generics model** towards an **R&D-driven, niche therapies-focused business**, reflecting the maturity of its legacy growth model.
- **Strategic Acquisition:** The **BSV acquisition** provides a strong entry into the **specialty biologics** segment, enhancing the company's presence in high-value therapies.
- **Growth Strategy:** InCred noted that **recent growth has been driven more by portfolio acquisitions than organic volume expansion**, with the benefits of the transformation expected to materialize gradually over time.

**Key Takeaway**
- InCred is **constructive on Mankind Pharma's long-term prospects**, believing its **strategic shift toward specialty therapies, R&D and biologics** positions the company for sustainable growth, although the full benefits are expected to unfold gradually.

**CLSA on Industrials**

Indian government has granted a two-year exemption to four Chinese electrical equipment manufacturers with manufacturing units in India, allowing them to participate in government/CPSE tenders.

More important is that this exemption was granted at request of Ministry of Power, validating case led by complaints from transmission utilities struggling with price inflation on controlled supply chains

Do not see this move impacting near-term volume growth, but increased competition (ex-HVDC converters) is likely to weigh on pricing power & margins of domestic T&D equipment cos such as Hitachi, GE Vernova T&D, BHEL (U-PF) & CG Power

Investors should be wary of lofty valuations (PE of 50-90x) on historic peak high margins led by ‘Make in India” driven shield but now govt seems to have taken cognisance of excessive margins being made by transformer sector.

**Stock Stats | Stock info | Infographics
****@Stockinfo333**

**Macquarie on Industrials**

As per industry sources, government is set to exempt 4 Chinese players (detailed below), having manufacturing units in India, from the restriction imposed in February 2023 for critical power projects.

This exemption is valid for a period of two years (till June 2028)

Most of grid companies are in midst of capacity expansion and are likely to add capacity over the next 2-3 year, which is the validity of this exemption.

Should this exemption be to extend in future, long-term negative impact on margins of the incumbents, especially the 765kV GIS players cannot be ruled out

Barring TBEA, most of other companies have sub-scale operations in India & are likely to take time to ramp up

Believe this exemption is to address current shortage of certain of EHV grid equipment & unlikely to allow large scale Chinese imports in sensitive areas of grid infrastructure.

****@Stockinfo333**

Jefferies on Industrials

Media reports highlight that government has approved four Chinese companies (TBEA Energy, Nanjing Electric, New Northeast Electric, Taikai Electric) to supply equipment for government projects only from their Indian manufacturing facilities.

Import restrictions still remain.

Even with supply from these facilities, believe demand-supply mismatch remains.

Remain positive on Hitachi Energy and Siemens Energy and see the correction as a buying opportunity

Global Semiconductor Supply Chain Remains Highly Concentrated

Key Highlights:

🏭 TSMC controls over 80% of the world's leading-edge (<16nm) chip production, powering most AI servers and premium smartphones.

🌏 Taiwan, South Korea, and China account for ~85% of global semiconductor foundry revenue, highlighting regional concentration.

💾 Samsung and SK Hynix produce ~70% of the world's DRAM memory chips.

⚙️ ASML holds a near-100% monopoly in EUV lithography, the critical technology required to manufacture chips below 7nm.

🇺🇸 US companies generate around 60% of global chip design revenue, while China accounts for ~38% of global OSAT (chip packaging and testing) capacity.

🏗️ 56% of global wafer capacity still lies in mature nodes (≥34nm), creating an opportunity for emerging semiconductor manufacturing hubs like India.
